empty_states.description: The screen a user sees when there is no data yet, no results, or after clearing content. One of the most overlooked surfaces.

anatomy.rule: Never just show a blank surface or a generic 'No data' label. Tell the user why it's empty and what to do next.

types.context: User has just created an account or project — nothing exists yet
types.message_pattern: Start by creating your first [X]. It only takes a minute.
types.tone: Inviting, not intimidating. Show the possibility.
types.icon_style: Friendly illustration or large soft icon in accent-subtle bg

types.context: User searched or filtered and got no results
types.message_pattern: Try different keywords or clear the filters.
types.icon_style: Search icon or magnifying glass, neutral tone

types.context: User completed all tasks, cleared inbox, or resolved all issues
types.cta: Optional — browse, explore, or simply omit
types.tone: Celebratory or calm. User achieved something.

types.context: Content failed to fetch — network error, server error
types.message_pattern: We couldn't load [X]. Check your connection or try again.
types.tone: Honest but not alarming. Offer recovery path.

types.context: User doesn't have access to this content
types.headline_pattern: You don't have access to this
types.message_pattern: Contact your admin or upgrade your plan to unlock [X].
types.tone: Clear about the blocker, not apologetic.

rules: Always include at least a headline + one action on empty states where action is possible
NEVER use generic placeholder text like 'No data available' — name the content type
Search empty states must always offer a way to clear/reset
First-time empty states are a sales moment — highlight the value of adding content
Error empty states must always offer a retry action

loading_states.when_to_use_skeleton: For content with a known structure (cards, tables, list items) — skeletons prevent layout shift and set expectations
loading_states.when_to_use_spinner: For actions with unknown duration or no spatial location (saving, submitting a form, API calls triggered by button)
loading_states.when_to_use_progress_bar: For file uploads, multi-step processes, or long operations where progress can be measured (0–100%)
loading_states.when_to_use_inline_indicator: For optimistic UI — show 'Saving...' text inline near the changed element, not globally
skeleton_rules: Match skeleton dimensions to actual content as closely as possible — prevents layout shift on load
Use shimmer animation (gradient moving left-to-right) at 1.5–2s loop, ease-in-out
Never use skeleton for content that loads in under 200ms — just show content directly
Show skeleton immediately, not after a delay — perceived performance improvement
spinner_rules: Global loading spinner blocks the entire UI — use only for full-page navigations or critical blocking operations
Button spinner: replace button label with spinner + 'Loading...' text, keep button width stable
NEVER show both a skeleton and a spinner at the same time for the same content
Spinner size: 16px inline, 20px button, 32px section, 48px page-level
progress_bar_rules: Always show percentage or step count alongside the bar
Indeterminate (bouncing) progress bar: only when progress cannot be measured
File upload: show file name + size + cancel button alongside progress bar
Animate fill with ease-in-out, not linear — progress should feel responsive

optimistic_ui.pattern: Apply state change immediately on user action, then confirm or revert based on server response
optimistic_ui.use_for: Likes, follows, toggles, reorder by drag, mark-as-read, archive
optimistic_ui.revert_pattern: On error: revert to previous state + show toast 'Couldn't save — changes undone'
optimistic_ui.rule: Always provide a manual undo option for optimistic actions that are hard to reverse

types.placement: Below the invalid field, immediately after submission or on field blur
types.color: danger-subtle bg + danger-border + danger icon + danger text
types.example: Email address is invalid. Enter a valid email like name@example.com.
types.rule: Never group all errors at the top of the form. Show each error next to its field.

types.placement: Replace the content area with the error state (see empty states — error type)
types.rule: Show error in place of the content, not as an overlay. Preserve navigation.

types.duration: Persistent — error toasts should NOT auto-dismiss. User must acknowledge.
types.role: role='alert' (assertive live region — screen reader announces immediately)
types.rule: Use for background operation failures (auto-save failed, sync failed) that the user didn't trigger directly

types.placement: Inside the component (e.g., data table row that failed to save, card that couldn't update)
types.color: Danger icon + danger text, no background change on the whole card
types.rule: Keep error message within 60 characters — be specific about what failed

required_elements: What to do next (2–3 options: home, back, search)
types.rule: Never show a stack trace or technical error in production. Show human language always.
Redirect is best for irreversible submissions (payment, order). Toast for reversible or small actions.

types.pattern: Inline 'Saved' text with checkmark near the save button, disappears after 2–3s
types.rule: Keep it subtle — don't celebrate a routine auto-save. Reserve toasts for manual saves.

types.pattern: Success illustration + celebratory headline + clear next step
types.rule: Onboarding completion, first project created, first payment received — these deserve a moment. Use accent color, not danger/warning.
